mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of t-1mo suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of t-1mo, near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market inflection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations. Combined with falling housing starts, this dual demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 723853M as of t-2mo, HOUST at 1366K as of t-2mo
regime_hint: demand shock regime with synchronized consumption and housing contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of dual-trigger instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines signal reduced manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness, indicating deteriorating corporate profitability and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines show no statistical difference from baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines signal labor market weakness and reduced aggregate income, which compresses consumer spending and corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the earnings recession deepens.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with employment contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation measured by CPI combined with high federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 319.775 as of t-1mo, FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of dual-trigger instances, or the interaction term (inflation × rates) shows no incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth declines signal weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward in response to demand weakness.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-growth quarters show no statistical difference from high-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline combined with retail sales weakness signals synchronized contraction in both residential investment and consumer spending, indicating a demand shock that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 50K persons
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1366K, RSAFS at 723853M, PAYEMS at 159218K as of t-2mo or t-1mo
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock with housing and consumption contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of triple-trigger instances, or the three-way interaction shows no incremental predictive power beyond pairwise interactions (F-test p > 0.20).
